content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
January 25,
1993
James M.
Roth,
Esq.
Jensen
& Roth
Attorneys at Law
The Escondido Financial Center
451 South Escondido Boulevard
Escondido,
California
92025
Dear Mr.
Roth:
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
G-93-31
I
am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
January 25,
1993,
wherein we discussed your request for a
Commission opinion under Government Code Section 83114(a)
of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1
.
I informed you that pursuant
to Regulation 18320(f) (7)
(copy enclosed),
your request falls
outside the scope of the Act.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner,
General
Co nsel
By:
ounsel,
Legal Division
JM:JS:dl
enc.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et .
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
I
P.O. Box 807
I
Sacramento, CA
95812 I
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a matter falls within the scope of the Political Reform Act and qualifies for a Commission opinion under Government Code Section 83114(a).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the requested matter falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act pursuant to Regulation 18320(f)(7), and therefore does not qualify for a Commission opinion.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; content is minimal and conveyed primarily through correspondence format. Letter confirms prior telephone discussion rather than providing detailed written analysis. Regulation 18320(f)(7) cited as basis for declination but not reproduced in document body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a matter falls within the scope of the Political Reform Act and qualifies for a Commission opinion under Government Code Section 83114(a).

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined that the requested matter falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act pursuant to Regulation 18320(f)(7), and therefore does not qualify for a Commission opinion.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission January 25, 1993 James M. Roth, Esq. Jensen & Roth Attorneys at Law The Escondido Financial Center 451 South Escondido Boulevard Escondido, California 92025 Dear Mr. Roth: Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. G-93-31 I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on January 25, 1993, wherein we discussed your request for a Commission opinion under Government Code Section 83114(a) of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 . I informed you that pursuant to Regulation 18320(f) (7) (copy enclosed), your request falls outside the scope of the Act. Sincerely, Jeff Marschner, General Co nsel By: ounsel, Legal Division JM:JS:dl enc. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et . All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 I P.O. Box 807 I Sacramento, CA 95812 I (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a request for a Commission opinion falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act. The determination was made following a telephone conversation on January 25, 1993.
